dataset: Synthetic employee performance review data (15 records, generated inline in setup_task.sh)

pi_items.source: Confirmed in task description, export_result.sh line 7 checks this exact path, and setup_task.sh line 13 deletes stale output at this path.

Parsed CSV from setup_task.sh lines 23-37: counted exactly 15 data rows (excluding header). Verified by Python csv.DictReader returning len(rows)==15.

Verified each placeholder exists in the template Python code (setup_task.sh lines 55-75): [FIRST_NAME] at line 55, [LAST_NAME] at line 55, [DEPARTMENT] at line 59, [GOALS_COMPLETED] at line 67, [GOALS_TARGET] at line 67, [ATTENDANCE_RATE] at line 67, [SALARY_ADJUSTMENT] at line 69, [MANAGER_NAME] at line 75. All 8 present. Note: placeholder names use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE while CSV column names use CamelCase (e.g., [FIRST_NAME] maps to FirstName column).

Parsed CSV and filtered rows where Rating=='Exceeds Expectations'. Found exactly 5 matches: Sarah Chen (row 1), Priya Sharma (row 5), Olivia Jackson (row 7), Nathan Williams (row 10), Rebecca O'Brien (row 14). Sorted lists match.

Parsed CSV and filtered rows where Rating=='Meets Expectations'. Found exactly 7 matches: Michael Thompson (row 2), James Kowalski (row 4), Daniel Mueller (row 6), Carlos Rivera (row 8), Elena Petrova (row 11), Marcus Brown (row 12), Thomas Andersen (row 15). Sorted lists match.
Parsed CSV and filtered rows where Rating=='Needs Improvement'. Found exactly 3 matches: Amara Okafor (row 3), Yuki Tanaka (row 9), Aisha Hassan (row 13). Sorted lists match.

Parsed CSV and filtered rows where SalaryAdjustment=='0'. Found exactly 5 matches: Amara Okafor (row 3, NI), Daniel Mueller (row 6, Meets), Yuki Tanaka (row 9, NI), Elena Petrova (row 11, Meets), Aisha Hassan (row 13, NI). Sorted lists match. Distribution: 3 Needs Improvement + 2 Meets Expectations = 5 total.

privileged_info_summary: The merged output document (performance_reviews_final.odt) should contain 15 personalized performance review letters separated by page breaks. The CSV data source has 9 columns (FirstName, LastName, Department, Rating, GoalsCompleted, GoalsTarget, AttendanceRate, SalaryAdjustment, ManagerName) and 15 rows. The template contains 8 placeholders in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE ([FIRST_NAME], [LAST_NAME], [DEPARTMENT], [GOALS_COMPLETED], [GOALS_TARGET], [ATTENDANCE_RATE], [SALARY_ADJUSTMENT], [MANAGER_NAME]) which must be replaced with merge fields mapped to the CamelCase CSV column names. Three conditional paragraphs control rating-specific text: 'Your performance this year has been exceptional...' appears only for Exceeds Expectations employees (Sarah Chen, Priya Sharma, Olivia Jackson, Nathan Williams, Rebecca O'Brien); 'You have met the expectations...' appears only for Meets Expectations employees (Michael Thompson, James Kowalski, Daniel Mueller, Carlos Rivera, Elena Petrova, Marcus Brown, Thomas Andersen); 'Your performance this year has fallen below...' appears only for Needs Improvement employees (Amara Okafor, Yuki Tanaka, Aisha Hassan). The salary paragraph ('Based on your performance, a salary adjustment of $...') should be hidden when SalaryAdjustment is 0, affecting 5 employees: Amara Okafor ($0), Daniel Mueller ($0), Yuki Tanaka ($0), Elena Petrova ($0), Aisha Hassan ($0). Non-zero salary employees receive: Sarah Chen $5,500, Michael Thompson $2,000, James Kowalski $1,500, Priya Sharma $4,800, Olivia Jackson $6,000, Carlos Rivera $1,800, Nathan Williams $5,200, Marcus Brown $2,500, Rebecca O'Brien $4,500, Thomas Andersen $1,200. Specific data points for spot-checking: Sarah Chen completed 12/10 goals with 98% attendance in Engineering under James Rodriguez; Aisha Hassan completed 3/10 goals with 78% attendance in Engineering under James Rodriguez; Thomas Andersen completed 8/10 goals with 93% attendance in Finance under Sophia Adams.
